description:
lib.rs:99 calls std::fs::read(parsed.path()) and wraps the *returned* Vec in Zeroizing. std::fs::read internally grows its buffer as it reads — when the buffer reallocates, the previous allocation containing partial key bytes is freed without being zeroed. The Zeroizing wrapper only zeroes the final Vec at drop, not the intermediate buffers freed during read.

For a private signing key this is a higher-impact version of the same bug already filed against secretx-file as qth2.3. The secret bytes that may leak are PKCS#8 DER encodings of Ed25519/P-256/RSA-2048 private keys.

Fix: pre-size the buffer using std::fs::metadata().len() and read into it via File::read_to_end, OR use std::fs::File + read_to_end on a pre-allocated Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>. The latter matches the workspace fix pattern proposed for qth2.3.

Test oracle: not easily testable in unit tests (would need allocator instrumentation or libfuzzer-style coverage). Document as 'best-effort zeroization' if the fix is not taken — but prefer the fix.
title: [bug] from_uri reads key via std::fs::read — non-zeroizing Vec growth may leave secret key bytes in deallocated heap pages
